name: Hallway
description: A corridor connecting two rooms. Floor of @floor, two lateral walls of @primary along the longer horizontal axis, and a flat @slab ceiling enclosing the passage. The two short ends are open so the hallway acts as a connector. Alternating @light blocks sit under the ceiling every 3-4 blocks; one or two @carpet runners line the floor; optional flower-pot niches every 5 blocks decorate one side wall.

required_furniture.rule: Solid @floor plane at y0 spanning the full AABB footprint
Two @primary lateral walls along the longer axis; short ends remain open
Flat @slab plane at y_top covering the corridor (beams of @primary every 2 blocks for fantasy long halls)
Alternating @light blocks every 3-4 blocks along the ceiling centreline; at least 2 lights regardless of length
One centred runner for narrow corridors (interior width <= 2), two parallel runners for wider ones
Optional niche decoration; one pot every 5 blocks along one side wall when length >= 6

placement_rules: Carpet runners centred for narrow halls, edge-aligned for wider ones
Flower-pot niches only on the inner face of one side wall, at floor+1
Ceiling slabs sit at y_top so a roof or upper-storey skill does not collide with the corridor cavity
lighting_ventilation: Ceiling-mounted lights every 3-4 blocks (torches on medieval, redstone lamps on modern, sea lanterns on fantasy). Hallway is ventilated by the open short ends connecting to adjacent rooms; no windows by default since lateral walls are short.

medieval.notes: Cobblestone floor, oak-plank lateral walls, oak-slab ceiling, red carpet runner, wall torches every 3 blocks.

modern.notes: Quartz walls, andesite floor, smooth-stone-slab ceiling, gray carpet runners, redstone-lamp ceiling lights.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak walls, mossy-cobblestone floor, dark-oak beams every 2 blocks instead of a flat ceiling for long halls, purple carpet runner, sea-lantern lights.
